enrichment.short_description: AI sales platform that finds prospects via buying signals and automates personalized outreach across email, phone, and social.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Amplemarket: AI sales platform for signal-based prospecting, multichannel outreach, and email deliverability monitoring.

features.title: Duo Copilot
features.description: AI sales agent that captures signals like website visits, job changes, and competitor activity, then builds personalized multichannel sequences.

features.title: Searcher
features.description: Prospect search tool with filtering to build targeted lead lists.

features.title: Intent signals
features.description: Tracks job changes, competitive activity, and website engagement to surface buying intent.

features.title: Data enrichment & email validation
features.description: Enriches contact records and validates emails to reduce bounce rates before sending.

features.title: Multichannel sequences
features.description: Automates outreach across email, phone, and social channels.

features.title: Unibox
features.description: Unified inbox for managing replies and conversations across channels.

features.title: Domain Health Center
features.description: Monitors email warmup, spam risk, and inbox placement to protect deliverability.

pros: Combines prospecting, enrichment, outreach, and deliverability monitoring in one platform.
Duo Copilot automates signal-based sequence creation across multiple channels.
Domain Health Center gives visibility into deliverability before sending campaigns.
Recognized by Gartner as a "Generative AI Cool Vendor."
cons: Entry-level Startup plan is priced at $600/month on an annual term, with no published month-to-month rate.
Growth and Elite plans require contacting sales for custom pricing.
No free plan; only a free trial is offered.
Founding year and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: Does Amplemarket offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No published free plan; Amplemarket offers a free trial rather than an ongoing free tier.

faqs.question: What is Duo Copilot?
faqs.answer: Duo Copilot is Amplemarket's AI sales agent that captures buying signals and builds personalized multichannel outreach sequences.

faqs.question: What channels can I use for outreach?
faqs.answer: Amplemarket supports automated sequences across email, phone/voice, and social, managed from a unified Unibox.

faqs.question: How much does Amplemarket cost?
faqs.answer: The Startup plan is $600/month on an annual term for 2 users; Growth and Elite plans use custom pricing.

faqs.question: Does Amplemarket help with email deliverability?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Domain Health Center monitors warmup, spam risk, and inbox placement.

use_cases.title: Signal-based outbound prospecting
use_cases.description: Finds prospects using buying signals and builds automated, personalized sequences.

use_cases.title: Unified multichannel campaigns
use_cases.description: Centralizes email, phone, and social outreach in one workflow via Unibox.

use_cases.title: Early go-to-market outreach
use_cases.description: Uses AI-driven signals to find and reach first customers without a large sales team.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Amplemarket?
paragraphs: Amplemarket is an AI sales platform that helps teams find prospects, track buying signals, and run automated outreach across email, phone, and social channels.
Its Duo Copilot analyzes signals such as website visits, job changes, and competitor activity to build personalized multichannel sequences, while a Domain Health Center monitors email deliverability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Amplemarket is for
paragraphs: Amplemarket is built for sales reps, sales leaders, RevOps teams, and founders who want prospecting, enrichment, and multichannel outreach in a single platform.

enrichment.short_description: Go-to-market platform that unifies buyer data and signals with AI agents (RoomieAI) to help revenue teams prioritize prospects.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Common Room: AI go-to-market platform unifying CRM data and buyer signals with RoomieAI agents for prospecting.

features.title: Context360
features.description: Unifies first-party data from CRM, product, marketing, and engagement tools with external buyer signals.

features.title: RoomieAI
features.description: AI agents that handle account research, contact research, and message personalization.

features.title: Signals
features.description: Tracks job changes, website visits, dark-funnel activity, and product-led sales signals.

features.title: Person360
features.description: Waterfall enrichment and identity resolution across a stated 400M+ contact directory.

features.title: Revenue Control Plane
features.description: Workflow automation and governance tools for GTM teams.

features.title: DataAgent
features.description: Validates and maintains CRM data alignment in real time.

features.title: Embedded Activation
features.description: Integrates with Salesforce, Slack, email, and browser extensions.

pros: Combines first-party CRM/product data with external buying signals in one record via Context360.
RoomieAI agents automate account and contact research plus message personalization.
Draws from a stated 400M+ contact directory for enrichment and identity resolution.
Named enterprise customers include Squarespace, Zendesk, Atlassian, Okta, Notion, MongoDB, and HubSpot.
cons: Pricing above the Essential tier ($2,500/month) is custom/quote-only.
No free plan is advertised; access requires a demo request.
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: What is RoomieAI?
faqs.answer: RoomieAI is Common Room's set of AI agents that handle account research, contact research, and outreach personalization.

faqs.question: How much does Common Room cost?
faqs.answer: The Essential plan is $2,500/month billed annually for 5 seats; Advanced and Enterprise tiers use custom pricing.

faqs.question: What data does Common Room use?
faqs.answer: It unifies first-party data (CRM, product usage, marketing, engagement) with buyer signals like job changes and website visits via Context360.

faqs.question: Does Common Room integrate with my CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with CRM, sales engagement platforms, and Slack, among 50+ listed integrations.

use_cases.title: Account-based prospecting
use_cases.description: Uses unified buyer signals and AI research agents to identify and engage target accounts.

use_cases.title: CRM data hygiene
use_cases.description: Keeps CRM records validated and aligned in real time via DataAgent.

use_cases.title: ABM scoring
use_cases.description: Identifies and scores accounts using Person360 and Context360 data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Common Room?
paragraphs: Common Room is a go-to-market platform that unifies CRM, product, and marketing data with external buyer signals into a single view of each prospect.
Its RoomieAI agents handle account and contact research and message personalization, while Person360 provides waterfall enrichment across a stated 400M+ contact directory.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Common Room is for
paragraphs: Common Room targets SDRs, account executives, RevOps, and ABM teams that need unified buyer intelligence across CRM and marketing systems.

enrichment.short_description: Sales intelligence platform that flags buying signals like job changes and uses an AI agent (Gem-E) to score and prioritize prospects.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: UserGems: AI-driven signal tracking and Gem-E scoring to prioritize outbound and ABM prospects.

features.title: Signals
features.description: Captures 30+ native buying signals including job changes, contact-level intent, website visits, M&A activity, and hiring data.

features.title: Gem-E
features.description: AI agent that analyzes 600+ signals and learns from closed-won patterns to build a customizable scoring model.

features.title: Orchestration
features.description: Coordinates outreach, ads, and CRM updates across sales and marketing channels simultaneously.

features.title: Research Agent
features.description: Discovers custom signals beyond native integrations.

features.title: CRM/sales-tool integration
features.description: Works within Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, and Salesloft.

pros: Gem-E's scoring model is described as transparent and customizable rather than a black box.
Tracks 30+ native buying signals, including job changes and M&A activity, without custom setup.
Offers a money-back guarantee tied to revenue generated from spend.
Integrates directly with major CRM and sales engagement tools.
cons: Entry-level Core plan starts at $2,750/month ($33,000/year) plus a one-time implementation fee.
Several capabilities (intent signals, research agent, de-anonymization) are add-ons on lower tiers.
No free plan; pricing is enterprise-oriented.
Founding year and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: What is Gem-E?
faqs.answer: Gem-E is UserGems' AI agent that analyzes signals and learns from closed-won deals to score and prioritize prospects.

faqs.question: How much does UserGems cost?
faqs.answer: The Core plan starts at $2,750/month ($33,000/year); Advanced and Elite tiers cost $5,750/month and $10,000/month respectively.

faqs.question: Is there a smaller plan for startups?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Starter Package is available for companies under 80 employees, priced on request.

faqs.question: What signals does UserGems track?
faqs.answer: 30+ signals including job changes, intent data, website visits, M&A activity, and hiring changes.

use_cases.title: Champion job-change tracking
use_cases.description: Alerts reps when a past buyer or champion moves to a new company.

use_cases.title: Signal-based ABM orchestration
use_cases.description: Coordinates outreach and ads across sales and marketing channels using buying signals.

use_cases.title: Pipeline prioritization
use_cases.description: Scores prospects using Gem-E's closed-won-based model.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is UserGems?
paragraphs: UserGems is a sales intelligence platform that tracks buying signals, such as job changes, intent data, and M&A activity, to help outbound and ABM teams find warm prospects.
Its Gem-E AI agent analyzes signals against closed-won patterns to build a customizable scoring model, then helps orchestrate outreach across sales and marketing channels.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who UserGems is for
paragraphs: UserGems is built for enterprise sales, ABM, and RevOps teams running outbound and account-based programs on top of Salesforce, HubSpot, Outreach, or Salesloft.

enrichment.short_description: Identifies website visitors at the person level and engages them via an AI chatbot, popups, and ad retargeting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Warmly: person-level website visitor identification with AI chat, intent scoring, and ad retargeting.

features.title: Inbound Agent
features.description: Person-level website visitor identification with an AI chatbot and smart popups based on intent.

features.title: TAM Agent
features.description: Dynamic audience building with AI ICP tiering, buying-committee identification, and ML intent scoring.

features.title: Context Graph
features.description: Unified data layer combining site visits, emails, meetings, and CRM data for agent context.

features.title: Intent signals
features.description: Tracks 1st-party (site/product/CRM), 2nd-party (social/hiring), and 3rd-party (keyword/competitor) signals.

features.title: Meeting booking & lead routing
features.description: Automated scheduling and routing from identified visitors.

features.title: Ad retargeting
features.description: Syncs identified visitors to LinkedIn and email retargeting campaigns.

pros: Combines website de-anonymization, AI chat, and ad retargeting in one platform.
TAM Agent adds ML-based intent scoring and automated buying-committee mapping.
Accessible via app, API, and MCP for custom workflows.
Reports a 4.8/5 rating from 200+ reviews (per the vendor's own site).
cons: Plans are priced from $10,000 to $30,000/year, with no published free tier.
Full autopilot capabilities are reserved for the top AI Inbound Autopilot plan.
Add-ons like the GTM Signals Package and Warm Experiences cost extra ($10,000/year each).
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: What does Warmly's Inbound Agent do?
faqs.answer: It identifies website visitors at the person level and engages them via an AI chatbot, smart popups, and retargeting.

faqs.question: How much does Warmly cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $10,000/year for AI Web-Deanonymization, up to $30,000/year for AI Inbound Autopilot; quarterly billing is also available.

faqs.question: Can Warmly identify who visits my site?
faqs.answer: Yes, its core capability is person-level website visitor identification.

faqs.question: Does Warmly have an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, the platform is accessible via app, API, and MCP.

use_cases.title: Inbound lead capture
use_cases.description: Identifies and engages anonymous website visitors in real time.

use_cases.title: Account-based outbound
use_cases.description: Builds and scores target account lists with the TAM Agent.

use_cases.title: Pipeline acceleration
use_cases.description: Combines intent signals with automated outreach.

features: AI chatbot with 1 AI Studio Agent

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Warmly?
paragraphs: Warmly is a B2B go-to-market platform that identifies website visitors at the person level and engages them through an AI chatbot, popups, and retargeting.
Its TAM Agent extends this with AI-driven account tiering, intent scoring, and buying-committee identification, fed by a shared Context Graph of site, email, and meeting data.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Warmly is for
paragraphs: Warmly is aimed at marketing, SDR, and RevOps teams that want to convert anonymous website traffic into identified, engaged pipeline.

enrichment.short_description: Go-to-market data platform combining waterfall enrichment from 200+ providers with AI research agents (Claygents).
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Clay: waterfall data enrichment and AI research agents for prospecting, scoring, and outreach automation.

features.title: Claygents
features.description: AI agents that research target companies and people using web data.

features.title: Waterfall Enrichment
features.description: Pulls from 200+ data providers in a single marketplace to fill contact and company records.

features.title: Signals & Intent
features.description: Tracks job changes, promotions, and engagement signals.

features.title: Audiences
features.description: Centralizes first- and third-party data and syncs it to LinkedIn, Meta, and Google Ads.

features.title: Sequencer
features.description: Native email sequencing and automation.

features.title: CRM Enrichment
features.description: Continuously refreshes CRM records for accuracy.

features.title: Account/Lead Scoring
features.description: AI-powered prioritization of accounts and leads.

pros: Free plan available with 500 actions and 100 data credits per month, unlimited seats and tables.
Waterfall enrichment draws from 200+ data providers in one marketplace.
Claygents let users build custom AI research workflows without code.
Usage-based pricing (actions/data credits) scales incrementally rather than jumping straight to enterprise tiers.
cons: Free plan caps tables at 200 rows, well below paid tiers.
CRM auto-sync, webhook automation, and ad audience sync require the Growth plan or above.
Enterprise features (SSO, RBAC, data warehouse integrations) require a custom annual contract.
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the pages reviewed.

faqs.question: Does Clay have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 500 actions/month, 100 data credits/month, and unlimited seats and tables (up to 200 rows per table).

faqs.question: What are Claygents?
faqs.answer: Claygents are Clay's AI research agents that gather company and people data from the web.

faqs.question: How is Clay priced?
faqs.answer: Clay uses tiered plans (Free, Launch, Growth, Enterprise) plus usage-based actions and data credits; Launch starts around $167/month.

faqs.question: Can Clay sync data to ad platforms?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Audiences feature syncs first- and third-party data to LinkedIn, Meta, and Google Ads.

faqs.question: Does Clay offer a trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day Pro trial is offered with no credit card required.

use_cases.title: Prospect enrichment
use_cases.description: Waterfalls through 200+ providers to fill missing contact and company data.

use_cases.title: AI-assisted account research
use_cases.description: Uses Claygents to gather pre-call research automatically.

use_cases.title: CRM hygiene
use_cases.description: Continuously refreshes and enriches CRM records.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Clay?
paragraphs: Clay is a go-to-market data platform that combines waterfall enrichment across 200+ data providers with AI research agents (Claygents) for prospecting and account research.
It also includes a native email sequencer, intent-signal tracking, and audience syncing to ad platforms like LinkedIn, Meta, and Google.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Clay is for
paragraphs: Clay serves GTM operations, SDRs, and marketing teams who want custom enrichment and outreach workflows on usage-based pricing rather than a single fixed data source.

enrichment.short_description: B2B prospecting tool that finds and verifies work emails and phone numbers in real time, with a Chrome extension and job-change alerts.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Wiza: real-time email and phone verification, Chrome extension, and job-change alerts for B2B prospecting.

features.title: Real-time prospecting
features.description: Checks live data sources at the moment of a search rather than serving stale cached records.

features.title: Chrome extension
features.description: One-click email and phone discovery with bulk export to CSV or CRM.

features.title: Wiza Monitor
features.description: Alerts users when decision-makers or key contacts change jobs, with verified new contact data.

features.title: Wiza Prospect
features.description: Search and filter across a stated 850M+ prospects with 40+ data points.

features.title: Email verification
features.description: Claims 99%+ deliverability rates on found emails.

features.title: CRM integration
features.description: Syncs found contacts with existing sales tools.

pros: Verifies emails in real time against live sources rather than a static database.
Wiza Monitor proactively alerts on job changes for tracked contacts.
Free plan available (20 valid emails, 5 phone numbers).
Chrome extension enables one-click lookups directly from prospect profile pages.
cons: Higher-tier plans (Email + Phone) run up to $199/month per user on monthly billing.
Phone number credits carry per-unit overage charges ($0.35 each) once monthly limits are exceeded.
Team plan pricing is custom and requires a 3+ user minimum.
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: Does Wiza have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 20 valid emails and 5 phone numbers.

faqs.question: What is Wiza Monitor?
faqs.answer: It's a feature that alerts users when tracked decision-makers or key contacts change jobs.

faqs.question: How much does Wiza cost?
faqs.answer: Individual plans range from $49/month (Starter) to $199/month (Email + Phone); annual billing lowers the per-month cost.

faqs.question: Does Wiza verify emails?
faqs.answer: Yes, it states 99%+ deliverability rates on the emails it returns.

use_cases.title: LinkedIn-based prospecting
use_cases.description: Finds verified contact data via the Chrome extension while browsing profiles.

use_cases.title: Job-change alerting
use_cases.description: Uses Wiza Monitor to flag when tracked contacts change roles or companies.

use_cases.title: Bulk contact list building
use_cases.description: Searches Wiza Prospect's 850M+ record database to build lead lists.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Wiza?
paragraphs: Wiza is a B2B prospecting tool that finds and verifies work emails and phone numbers in real time, pulling from live data sources rather than a static cache.
It includes a Chrome extension for one-click lookups, Wiza Prospect for bulk searching across a stated 850M+ records, and Wiza Monitor for job-change alerts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Wiza is for
paragraphs: Wiza is aimed at sales reps, recruiters, and marketers who need verified contact data for individual or bulk outreach lists.

enrichment.short_description: Contact-data platform that surfaces verified phone numbers and emails for sales and recruiting via a LinkedIn Chrome extension.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kaspr: LinkedIn Chrome extension for verified phone numbers and emails, built for sales and recruiting teams.

features.title: LinkedIn Chrome Extension
features.description: Gets contact data directly on a prospect's LinkedIn profile.

features.title: Bulk Data Enrichment
features.description: Updates lead lists with accurate contact information.

features.title: Dashboard
features.description: Centralized lead management with automation capabilities.

features.title: API access
features.description: Automated search functionality, full access on Business plan and above.

features.title: Sales Navigator / Recruiter Lite access
features.description: Included on paid tiers for LinkedIn power users.

pros: Free plan available with no credit card required to start.
Claims access to over 500 million verified phone numbers and emails from 150+ sources.
Supports English, German, Spanish, and French interfaces.
G2 rating of 4.4/5 from 750+ reviews (per the vendor's own site).
cons: Free plan is limited to 15 B2B email credits, 5 phone credits, and 5 direct email credits per month.
Full API access and intent data are reserved for Business/Enterprise tiers.
Lower per-month prices require annual billing; monthly billing costs more.
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: Is Kaspr free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kaspr offers a Free plan with 15 B2B email credits, 5 phone credits, and 5 direct email credits per month.

faqs.question: What does the Chrome extension do?
faqs.answer: It surfaces verified phone numbers and email addresses directly on a prospect's LinkedIn profile.

faqs.question: How much does Kaspr cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $49/month (Starter, billed annually) up to $79/month (Business), with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: What languages does Kaspr support?
faqs.answer: English, German (Deutsch), Spanish (Español), and French (Français).

use_cases.title: LinkedIn prospecting
use_cases.description: Surfaces verified contact details directly on LinkedIn profiles via the Chrome extension.

use_cases.title: Candidate sourcing
use_cases.description: Provides verified contact details for recruiting outreach.

use_cases.title: Lead list enrichment
use_cases.description: Bulk-updates lead lists with accurate contact information.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Kaspr?
paragraphs: Kaspr is a contact-data platform that surfaces verified phone numbers and email addresses for sales and recruiting, primarily through a LinkedIn Chrome extension.
It claims access to over 500 million verified phone numbers and emails from 150+ sources, with bulk enrichment and CRM/API integrations on paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kaspr is for
paragraphs: Kaspr targets sales teams, recruiters, and founders who prospect on LinkedIn and need verified direct-dial and email contact data.

enrichment.short_description: Email outreach platform for finding, verifying, and emailing business contacts via Domain Search, Email Finder, and Email Verifier.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Hunter: find, verify, and email business contacts with Domain Search, Email Finder, and Email Sequences.

features.title: Domain Search
features.description: Identifies contacts associated with a company's website or domain.

features.title: Email Finder
features.description: Locates a verified email address for a specific person by name and domain.

features.title: Email Verifier
features.description: Validates email addresses to reduce bounce rates.

features.title: Email Sequences
features.description: Sends personalized cold email campaigns at scale.

features.title: Browser extension
features.description: One-click email discovery while browsing.

features.title: AI Writing Assistant
features.description: Drafts outreach emails, available from the Starter plan upward.

pros: Free plan available (50 credits/month, 1 connected email account).
Combines finding, verifying, and emailing contacts in one platform.
Founded in 2015 with an established user base (7M+ users, 600k+ extension users, per the vendor's own site).
All paid plans include unlimited team members.
cons: Higher email-sequence recipient limits and more connected inboxes require upper tiers (Growth/Scale).
AI Writing Assistant is not available on the Free plan.
No stated headquarters location; the company describes itself as a distributed team.
Enterprise plan pricing is custom/quote-only.

faqs.question: When was Hunter founded?
faqs.answer: Hunter was created by Antoine Finkelstein and François Grante in 2015.

faqs.question: Does Hunter have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan includes 50 credits/month and 1 connected email account.

faqs.question: What does Hunter's Email Verifier do?
faqs.answer: It validates email addresses before sending to reduce bounce rates.

faqs.question: How much does Hunter cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at €49/month (Starter, or €34/month billed yearly) up to €299/month (Scale); Enterprise is custom.

faqs.question: Does Hunter offer an AI writing tool?
faqs.answer: Yes, an AI Writing Assistant is included from the Starter plan upward.

use_cases.title: Cold email prospecting
use_cases.description: Finds and verifies contact emails before sending sequences.

use_cases.title: Domain-based lead lists
use_cases.description: Pulls all known contacts for a target company via Domain Search.

use_cases.title: Deliverability protection
use_cases.description: Verifies lists before large sends to protect sender reputation.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Hunter?
paragraphs: Hunter is an email outreach platform that helps users find, verify, and email business contacts through Domain Search, Email Finder, and Email Verifier tools.
Founded in 2015, it also offers Email Sequences for scaled cold outreach and an AI Writing Assistant on paid plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Hunter is for
paragraphs: Hunter is built for sales professionals, business development teams, and marketers running B2B lead generation and cold email campaigns.

enrichment.short_description: AI-assisted lead generation platform for finding, verifying, and emailing B2B contacts through multichannel campaigns.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Snov.io: AI lead generation, email verification, and multichannel outreach with built-in CRM and warm-up tools.

features.title: Email Finder
features.description: AI-assisted lead discovery with verified emails from a B2B database.

features.title: Email Verifier
features.description: Validates emails with a stated 98% accuracy, including graylisting bypass.

features.title: LinkedIn Integration
features.description: Browser extension and LinkedIn search for prospect identification.

features.title: Outreach Automation
features.description: Multichannel campaigns combining email and LinkedIn with automated sequences.

features.title: Email Infrastructure
features.description: Domain and mailbox setup with automated inbox warm-up.

features.title: Sales CRM
features.description: Built-in deal tracking and pipeline management.

features.title: Deliverability tools
features.description: Inbox placement monitoring and spam-risk assessment.

pros: Free (Trial) plan available with 50 credits and 100 recipients, renewable.
Combines email finding, verification, warm-up, CRM, and multichannel outreach in one platform.
Supports 8 interface languages, including English, Spanish, German, French, and Chinese.
Integrates with 1,000+ tools via API plus native connectors (HubSpot, Pipedrive, Salesforce, Zapier, Make).
cons: Higher plans (Pro L, Ultra) run up to $738/month for larger sending volumes.
Advanced search limits and analytics are gated behind Pro-tier plans.
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site.

faqs.question: Does Snov.io have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a renewable Trial plan offers 50 credits and 100 recipients per month.

faqs.question: What languages does Snov.io support?
faqs.answer: English, Portuguese, Chinese (Simplified & Traditional), Ukrainian, Spanish, German, Polish, and French.

faqs.question: How much does Snov.io cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $39/month (Starter) and scale up to $738/month (Ultra), with custom pricing above that.

faqs.question: Does Snov.io integrate with my CRM?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with HubSpot, Pipedrive, Salesforce, Zapier, Make, and 1,000+ tools via API.

use_cases.title: Multichannel outbound
use_cases.description: Combines email and LinkedIn outreach in one automated sequence.

use_cases.title: Deliverability management
use_cases.description: Sets up sending domains and automated warm-up to protect sender reputation.

use_cases.title: Lead list building
use_cases.description: Uses the Email Finder and LinkedIn integration to build prospect lists.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Snov.io?
paragraphs: Snov.io is a lead generation and email outreach platform that finds and verifies B2B contacts, then automates multichannel campaigns across email and LinkedIn.
It also includes email infrastructure tools such as domain setup and automated warm-up, a built-in sales CRM, and deliverability monitoring.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Snov.io is for
paragraphs: Snov.io serves sales teams, agencies, and businesses running B2B lead generation and cold outreach across more than 180 countries.

name: RocketReach
skip_reason: Official site (rocketreach.co) renders as a JavaScript-only shell under automated fetch: the homepage, pricing page, and other subpaths returned only a page title with no usable body content, and further subpaths (person-search, faq) 404'd or redirected without content. Could not verify pricing, features, founding year, headquarters, or company details directly from the primary source, and third-party pricing summaries found via search conflict with each other on plan names and prices. Per the no-fabrication rule, skipping rather than guessing.
